title: Tampa Night 2 — BTS Performs on Day 4,700 Since Debut at Raymond James Stadium
detail: On April 26, 2026 — exactly 4,700 days since BTS's June 13, 2013 debut — all seven members perform the second of three sold-out nights at Raymond James Stadium in Tampa, Florida as part of the ARIRANG World Tour North America leg. Each night draws approximately 60,000–65,000 fans. Coverage published on April 26 by Korea Herald, Korea Times, STARNEWS Korea, and International Business Times confirms the full scope of the tour: 31 concerts across 12 North American cities, with additional dates added in Tampa, Stanford (CA), and Las Vegas due to demand. Next stops include El Paso at Sun Bowl Stadium (May 2–3, BTS's first show there) and Mexico City. Tampa Mayor Jane Castor lit city hall and downtown bridges in purple from Saturday through Wednesday. Economic impact for the Tampa region is projected at $800M–$900M by local and national media. Concerts run approximately 2 hours 10 minutes with all 7 members performing. US media coverage consistently describes the atmosphere as Beatlemania-like.

media.caption: BTS performs Night 2 of three sold-out ARIRANG World Tour shows at Raymond James Stadium, Tampa, on Day 4,700 since their 2013 debut

title: ARIRANG Tour: 31 Shows / 12 Cities Confirmed; 'SWIM' Climbs to New Peak No. 13 on Pop Radio Airplay
detail: On April 26, 2026, Soompi and Billboard report that 'SWIM' climbed to a new peak of No. 13 on Billboard's Pop Radio Airplay chart — BTS's highest-ever position on that survey and the highest ever by a Korean act. 'SWIM' simultaneously holds No. 1 on the Billboard Global 200 for a fourth consecutive week, No. 1 on Global Excl. U.S. (4th week), and No. 10 on the Billboard Hot 100. The album ARIRANG becomes the first album by a Korean artist to spend four consecutive weeks in the top 3 of the Billboard 200 (currently No. 2 after three weeks at No. 1). In addition, Billboard published the complete 23-song setlist from Tampa Night 1 on April 26, documenting the full sequence: Hooligan, Aliens, Run BTS (Remix), they don't know 'bout us, Like Animals, FAKE LOVE, SWIM, Merry Go Round, 2.0, NORMAL, Not Today, MIC Drop, FYA, Burning Up (Fire), Body to Body, IDOL, Come Over, Butter, Dynamite, Permission to Dance, Magic Shop, Please, Into the Sun.

media.caption: 'SWIM' reaches new peak No. 13 on Billboard Pop Radio Airplay — highest ever by a Korean act — as BTS performs Night 2 in Tampa
